descAuth: Enter your credentials to access your account
registerSubtitle: Fill in the details to register your store
secureSSLDesc: All data is encrypted using SSL technology
faReadyDesc: Our team is ready to help you anytime
resetPasswordDesc: Enter your email to receive a password reset link
tips.dashboard: Welcome to the dashboard — your business overview in one screen
tips.dashboard2: Monitor sales, low stock, and recent activity in real-time
tips.product: Add product photos to make items easier to identify at checkout
tips.product2: Use unique SKUs for each product variant for better stock management
tips.product3: Proper categories help with product search and accurate sales reports
tips.category: Categories are used to group similar products together
tips.category2: Every product must have a category for structured reporting
tips.supplier: Complete supplier data makes purchase orders easier
tips.supplier2: Save supplier contacts and addresses for faster communication
tips.member: Use the level filter to sort members by membership tier
tips.member2: Higher points mean higher member tiers — drive loyalty with attractive rewards
tips.memberTier: Tiers determine the rights and discounts members receive
tips.memberTier2: Set minimum points and benefits for each tier flexibly
tips.discount: Discounts can be applied per product or across an entire category
tips.discount2: Set discount validity periods to avoid exceeding promo limits
tips.payment: Configure payment methods available at the cashier
tips.payment2: Make sure each payment method has a valid destination account
tips.shift: Work shifts help track which cashier staff are on duty
tips.shift2: Schedule shifts before starting daily transactions
tips.purchaseOrder: Purchase orders are used to order stock from suppliers
tips.purchaseOrder2: Monitor PO status to avoid delivery delays
tips.stockOpname: Stock opname matches physical stock with system records
tips.stockOpname2: Perform stock opname regularly to maintain data accuracy
tips.stockHistory: Stock history records every change in product quantity
tips.stockHistory2: Use date filters to narrow down history searches
tips.lowStock: Low stock products need to be reordered soon
tips.lowStock2: Set minimum stock thresholds to receive automatic notifications
tips.expense: Record every business expense for accurate financial reports
tips.expense2: Categorize expenses by type for easier analysis
tips.employee: Employee data is used to manage access and payroll
tips.employee2: Make sure employee contact details and positions are always current
tips.department: Departments group employees by their function
tips.department2: Every employee must be registered under one department
tips.position: Positions determine access rights and employee responsibilities
tips.position2: Set up a hierarchical position structure for easier management
tips.table: Tables are used to track orders in restaurants or cafes
tips.tax: Configure applicable taxes for sales transactions
tips.tax2: Make sure the tax percentage matches current regulations
tips.priceList: Price list templates make creating product price lists easier
tips.priceList2: Use templates to print prices in various formats
tips.location: Store locations are used to manage stock and transactions per branch
tips.location2: Make sure addresses and contacts are always updated for invoice purposes
tips.user: Users with limited access rights to operate the system
tips.user2: Assign roles and permissions to each user based on their responsibilities
tips.report: Sales reports provide insights into business performance
tips.report2: Use date filters to compare sales across different periods
tips.invoice: Invoices are generated automatically after a transaction is completed
tips.invoice2: Customize invoice footer and logo for your store branding
tips.invoice3: Invoices can be sent directly to customers via WhatsApp
tips.cashier: The cashier page is used to process sales transactions
tips.cashier2: Scan product barcodes to speed up the checkout process
tips.cashier3: Payments can be processed with cash or non-cash methods
guide.auth.login.s1desc: This interactive guide will help you understand the Login page
guide.auth.login.s2desc: Enter your registered username or email address
guide.auth.login.s3desc: Password is case-sensitive. Watch your caps lock and numbers
guide.auth.login.s4desc: Check "Remember Me" to stay signed in. Click "Forgot Password?" if needed
guide.auth.login.s5desc: Click the Login button to access your dashboard based on your role
guide.auth.login.s6desc: Need help with another page? Choose one below:
guide.auth.register.s1desc: This guide will help you fill out the registration form
guide.auth.register.s2desc: Username must be at least 4 characters. Use an active email for verification
guide.auth.register.s3desc: Select your store location — this determines your branch access
guide.auth.register.s4desc: Password must be at least 4 characters. Make sure confirmation matches
guide.auth.register.s5desc: Click "Create Account" to complete your registration
guide.auth.register.s6desc: You now understand the basics of registration. Need more help?
guide.auth.reset.s1desc: This guide will help you reset your account password
guide.auth.reset.s2desc: Enter your registered email address for verification
guide.auth.reset.s3desc: New password must be at least 6 characters. Make sure confirmation matches
guide.auth.reset.s4desc: Click "Reset Password" to complete the process
guide.auth.reset.s5desc: You now understand how to reset your password. Need more help?
guide.auth.complete.desc: You now understand the basics of this page. Need further help?
guide.dashboard.s1desc: This tour will walk you through the header and dashboard, one by one. You can exit anytime using the close button.
guide.dashboard.s2desc: Quickly find any page or feature in the app. Click here or press ⌘K / Ctrl+K to open the command palette.
guide.dashboard.s3desc: Switch the interface language between Indonesian (ID) and English (EN). Your choice is saved for this session.
guide.dashboard.s4desc: Toggle between light and dark themes. The choice is applied instantly across the whole app.
guide.dashboard.s5desc: Klik tombol LifeBuoy di header atau tombol mengambang di kanan bawah untuk memulai tur panduan. Sidebar Support membuka halaman Bantuan.
guide.dashboard.s6desc: See real-time alerts for new orders, low stock, and system events. The red badge shows how many unread notifications you have.
guide.dashboard.s7desc: Access your profile settings or sign out of the system. Your role and username are shown here.
guide.dashboard.s8desc: Before this dashboard shows real data, complete the initial setup: add at least 1 store location, create products with categories, and (optionally) add members. This section displays 5 summary cards — Revenue (today's earnings + trend), Orders (total transactions), Active Products, Member count, and Low Stock (highlighted red when items need restocking). These cards are read-only; the chart and orders sections below contain the interactive buttons (Download Report, View All, Eye, Printer).
guide.dashboard.s9desc: Weekly revenue chart. Spot trends day by day and download the report for deeper analysis.
guide.dashboard.s10desc: List of the latest transactions. View status, totals, and order details, or print a receipt directly from here.
guide.location.s11adesc: Manage all your store locations from this page. In the next steps we'll highlight every element one by one: summary cards, the Add button, and the data table.
guide.location.s11bdesc: This card shows the total number of store locations registered in the system — both active and inactive combined.
guide.location.s11cdesc: Stores currently operational. The percentage below shows what portion of your total stores are active.
guide.location.s11ddesc: Stores marked as closed or paused. Click the card to filter the list down to only these stores.
guide.location.s11edesc: Number of unique cities where your stores operate. Useful for tracking your geographic reach.
guide.location.s11fdesc: Click this button to register a new store location. Fill in the store name, address, city, and manager.
guide.location.s11gdesc: All stores are listed here. Use the search bar above the table to filter by name or city. Each row has Detail, Edit, and Delete actions.
guide.category.s12adesc: Categories group your products (e.g. Food, Drinks, Snacks). Next we will walk through every element: the 3 summary cards, 4 header buttons, and the data table.
guide.category.s12bdesc: Total number of categories — both active and inactive combined.
guide.category.s12cdesc: Categories currently in use by your products. The percentage shows how many of your categories are active.
guide.category.s12ddesc: Categories that have been archived or have no products. Click the card to filter the list to inactive categories only.
guide.category.s12edesc: Downloads a blank Excel template with the correct column headers. Use this when you want to bulk-add categories via spreadsheet.
guide.category.s12fdesc: Exports the current list of categories (with images and product counts) as an Excel file for backup or reporting.
guide.category.s12gdesc: Bulk-import categories from an Excel file. Use the template above for the correct format.
guide.category.s12hdesc: Click to add a single new category. You'll be asked for a name, description, and image.
guide.category.s12idesc: Lists every category with its image, name, product count, and status. Use the search bar to filter and the row actions to Edit or Delete.
guide.supplier.s13adesc: Keep a list of all product suppliers. Next we will walk through every element: 3 summary cards, the Add button, the search bar, and the data table.
guide.supplier.s13bdesc: Total number of suppliers — active and inactive combined.
guide.supplier.s13cdesc: Suppliers you currently work with. The percentage shows the share of active suppliers.
guide.supplier.s13ddesc: Suppliers you no longer use. Click the card to filter the table to inactive suppliers only.
guide.supplier.s13edesc: Click to register a new supplier. Fill in name, contact person, phone, email, and address.
guide.supplier.s13fdesc: Filter the list by supplier name, contact person, or phone number. Results update as you type.
guide.supplier.s13gdesc: Lists every supplier with name, contact, phone, and address. Use the row actions to Edit or Delete.
guide.product.s14adesc: Your main product catalog. Next we will walk through every element: Import, Export, Add buttons, the search bar, and the data table.
guide.product.s14bdesc: Bulk-upload products from an Excel file. Use the template (available in the Category page) for the correct format.
guide.product.s14cdesc: Download the current product list as an Excel file for backup or offline review.
guide.product.s14ddesc: Click to add a single new product. Fill in SKU, name, category, price, stock, and image.
guide.product.s14edesc: Filter the catalog by SKU, product name, or barcode. Results update as you type.
guide.product.s14fdesc: Lists every product with SKU, name, category, price, stock (red = out, orange = low), status badge, and Edit/Delete actions.
guide.member.s15adesc: Your customer loyalty database. Next we will walk through the Add button, tier filter, search bar, and data table.
guide.member.s15bdesc: Register a new loyalty member. Fill in name, phone, email, and assign a tier (Bronze, Silver, Gold, etc).
guide.member.s15cdesc: Filter the list by member name, phone, or member ID. Results update as you type.
guide.member.s15ddesc: Lists every member with name, phone, point balance, tier badge, join date, and Edit/Delete actions.
guide.employee.s16adesc: Manage staff who use the system. Next we will walk through the 3 summary cards, Add button, and data table.
guide.employee.s16bdesc: Total number of employees registered — active and inactive combined.
guide.employee.s16cdesc: Employees currently with system access. The percentage shows the share of active staff.
guide.employee.s16ddesc: Employees who have been deactivated (resigned, on leave, etc). Click the card to filter the table to inactive staff only.
guide.employee.s16edesc: Register a new employee. Fill in name, email, password, role, and assign to a store location.
guide.employee.s16fdesc: Lists every employee with name, department, position, role, status, and Edit/Detail/Delete actions. Use the search bar to filter by name or position.
guide.department.s17adesc: Departments group employees (e.g. Kitchen, Front-of-House, Accounting). Next we will walk through every element: 4 stat cards, 4 header buttons, the search bar, and the data table.
guide.department.s17bdesc: Total number of departments registered in the system — both active and inactive.
guide.department.s17cdesc: Departments currently in use. The percentage shows the share of active departments.
guide.department.s17ddesc: Departments that are no longer in use. Click the card to filter the list down to inactive departments only.
guide.department.s17edesc: Departments that are missing a description. Add one to keep your org chart readable.
guide.department.s17fdesc: Downloads a blank Excel template with the correct column headers for bulk department import.
guide.department.s17gdesc: Exports the current list of departments as an Excel file for backup or reporting.
guide.department.s17hdesc: Bulk-import departments from an Excel file. Use the template above for the correct format.
guide.department.s17idesc: Click to register a new department. Fill in the name, description, and active status.
guide.department.s17jdesc: Filter the list by department name or description. Results update as you type.
guide.department.s17kdesc: Lists every department with name, description, status, created/updated dates, and View/Edit/Delete row actions.
guide.position.s18adesc: Positions are job titles within a department (e.g. Chef, Cashier, Manager). Next we will walk through every element: 3 stat cards, 4 header buttons, the search bar, and the data table.
guide.position.s18bdesc: Total number of positions registered — both active and inactive combined.
guide.position.s18cdesc: Positions currently in use. The percentage shows the share of active positions.
guide.position.s18ddesc: Positions that are no longer in use. Click the card to filter the list down to inactive positions only.
guide.position.s18edesc: Downloads a blank Excel template with the correct column headers for bulk position import.
guide.position.s18fdesc: Exports the current list of positions as an Excel file for backup or reporting.
guide.position.s18gdesc: Bulk-import positions from an Excel file. Make sure at least one department exists first.
guide.position.s18hdesc: Click to create a new position. Fill in the name, parent department, description, and active status.
guide.position.s18idesc: Filter the list by position name or department. Results update as you type.
guide.position.s18jdesc: Lists every position with name, parent department badge, description, and View/Edit/Delete row actions.
guide.settings.s19adesc: Customize how your printed/displayed invoice looks. Next we will walk through every element: Logo & Header, Store Address, Footer, the Save button, and the live preview.
guide.settings.s19bdesc: Upload your store logo and choose whether to show the logo and store name on the invoice header. Changes appear in the live preview on the right.
guide.settings.s19cdesc: Read-only summary of your store address (pulled from the store location). Toggle the switch to show or hide the address on the invoice.
guide.settings.s19ddesc: Type any text you want printed at the bottom of the invoice (e.g. return policy, thank-you message). Toggle the switch to show or hide the footer.
guide.settings.s19edesc: Click to save all invoice changes. The save button is enabled only when you have unsaved edits.
guide.settings.s19fdesc: Live preview of the invoice as it will appear when printed. Click the Print button at the top of the preview to print a sample receipt.
guide.tax.s20adesc: Configure tax rates (PPN, PPh, Non-Tax) that are applied to products and orders. Next we will walk through every element: 3 stat cards, the Add button, the search bar, and the data table.
guide.tax.s20bdesc: Total number of tax rates registered in the system — both active and inactive.
guide.tax.s20cdesc: Tax rates currently in use. The percentage shows the share of active tax rates.
guide.tax.s20ddesc: Tax rates that have been archived. Click the card to filter the list down to inactive rates only.
guide.tax.s20edesc: Click to add a new tax rate. Fill in the name, type (PPN, PPh, Non-Pajak), percentage, and description.
guide.tax.s20fdesc: Filter the list by tax name, type, or description. Results update as you type.
guide.tax.s20gdesc: Lists every tax rate with name, type badge, rate percentage, description, and Edit/Delete row actions.
guide.pricelist.s21adesc: Create reusable price tiers (e.g. Retail, Reseller, VIP) that can be applied to products. Next we will walk through every element: 3 stat cards, the Add button, the search bar, and the data table.
guide.pricelist.s21bdesc: Total number of price list templates registered in the system.
guide.pricelist.s21cdesc: Templates currently in use. The percentage shows the share of active templates.
guide.pricelist.s21ddesc: Templates that have been archived. Click the card to filter the list down to inactive templates only.
guide.pricelist.s21edesc: Click to create a new price list template. Add multiple price tiers with different names and price adjustments (e.g. +10%, -20%, fixed).
guide.pricelist.s21fdesc: Filter the list by template name or description. Results update as you type.
guide.pricelist.s21gdesc: Lists every template with name, tier badges (showing all price levels), description, and Edit/Delete row actions.
guide.role.s22adesc: Manage user roles and their access permissions. Each role defines what menus and actions users can see or do. Next we will walk through every element: the Add Role button and the role table.
guide.role.s22bdesc: Click to create a new role. You'll set the role name, description, and choose the access matrix (which menus and actions this role can use).
guide.role.s22cdesc: Lists every role with name, description, type badge (super_admin/admin/regular), active status, and Edit/Delete row actions. Click the edit icon to open the permission matrix and change which menus this role can access.
guide.tier.s25adesc: Configure loyalty tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold, etc) that group members by point balance. Each tier can have its own discount and benefits. Next we will walk through every element: 3 stat cards, the Add button, the search bar, and the tier table.
guide.tier.s25bdesc: Tiers currently in use with active status. Each tier has its own minimum-point threshold and discount rate.
guide.tier.s25cdesc: Total number of members currently assigned to any tier. Useful for tracking your loyalty program reach.
guide.tier.s25ddesc: The tier with the most growth in member count. Use this to identify which tier to promote or restructure.
guide.tier.s25edesc: Click to create a new tier. Set the name, color, minimum points required, discount percentage, and benefits list.
guide.tier.s25fdesc: Filter the list by tier name. Results update as you type.
guide.tier.s25gdesc: Lists every tier with id, color bar, name, minimum points, discount percentage, benefits badges, member count, and Edit/Delete actions.
guide.support.s27adesc: This is your one-stop support page. Find answers in the FAQ below, contact our team, or browse learning resources. Next we will walk through every section: contact methods, FAQ, and resources.
guide.support.s27bdesc: Send us a detailed message by email. Best for non-urgent questions and bug reports — we typically respond within 24 hours.
guide.support.s27cdesc: Call our customer support hotline for urgent issues. Available Monday-Friday 08:00-17:00 WIB.
guide.support.s27ddesc: Chat with our support team in real-time for quick questions. Best for short, immediate help.
guide.support.s27edesc: Browse common questions about the product, account, and features. Click any question to expand its answer.
guide.support.s27fdesc: Access our full user guide, video tutorials, and release notes. Links open in a new tab.
guide.dashboard.complete.desc: You've completed the dashboard tour. You can re-open this guide anytime via the Support button in the header.
